name: Atlas Credit Solutions
description_short: Credit repair firm that disputes errors and negative items on credit reports through a systematic 4-step process. Offers free consultations and aims to show results within 30 days.
description_long:
Atlas Credit Solutions (ACS) was established to help individuals restore their credit by analyzing and addressing factors that negatively impact credit scores. The company operates as a credit repair firm staffed with experts who specialize in disputing inaccuracies on credit reports and guiding clients through improvement strategies. Based on their website, ACS has helped thousands of families over many years in the credit industry.

ACS offers a structured credit repair process that begins with a free, no-impact soft inquiry to gather credit information, followed by a 15-minute expert review call. Their core service involves disputing errors with credit bureaus and providers through a two-step process (up to 30 days per step). They claim clients typically see initial results within the first 30 days, with complete processes potentially taking up to 70 days. The company works with all three major credit bureaus and positions itself as capable of challenging most negative items on credit reports.

ACS emphasizes competitive pricing, constant client communication, and detailed progress tracking. They highlight their ability to handle various credit situations with flexibility for different timelines. Their marketing focuses on the speed of results and the comprehensive nature of their analysis, with testimonials citing successful outcomes (e.g., a client getting mortgage approval within four months).

While ACS presents a professional credit repair operation, consumers should understand that credit repair itself is a regulated industry, and results depend heavily on the validity of disputed items. The website provides limited detail on pricing, guarantees, or specific success rates. Potential clients should verify credentials, understand legal limitations on what credit repair can accomplish, and review any fee structures before engaging.
address: 5825 Sunset Dr Suite 204, South Miami, FL 33143

company_info.headquarters: 5825 Sunset Dr Suite 204, South Miami, FL 33143

pros: Free initial consultation and soft credit inquiry with no impact on credit score
Transparent 4-step process with clear timelines (15-min review, 30 days per dispute step)
Claims to show initial results within first 30 days of starting process
Works with all three major credit bureaus simultaneously
Emphasizes competitive pricing and keeping client costs down
Provides ongoing updates and maintains accurate progress records
Handles various credit situations with flexible timelines
cons: Website provides no specific pricing information or fee structure details
No guarantees stated; claims about results (70-day timeline) are estimates only
Limited information about what constitutes 'results' or success rates
No details on company credentials, licensing, or compliance certifications
Testimonial sample is minimal (only one full review visible on website)
best_for: Consumers with errors or inaccuracies on their credit reports seeking professional disputes
Individuals denied credit or facing higher rates who need structured credit restoration
People wanting expert guidance on credit improvement strategies alongside dispute filing
diagnosis: Atlas Credit Solutions is best for consumers with legitimate errors or disputed negative items on their credit reports who want professional assistance filing disputes with credit bureaus. The main caveat is that credit repair results depend on whether items are actually inaccurate or disputable—the service cannot remove valid negative information, and the website lacks transparency on pricing, success rates, and regulatory compliance details that consumers should verify before engagement.
services: Free credit consultation and initial intake
Soft credit inquiry to pull credit history without impact to score
15-minute credit history review and expert analysis
Identification of factors affecting credit score
Dispute filing with credit bureaus and providers
Two-step dispute process management (up to 60 days total)
Work with all three major credit bureaus
